channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Taiwan's president departs for Pacific visit with a two-day stop in the US. China isn't happy
item.description: TAOYUAN, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan's president departed Saturday for his trip to the South Pacific that will include a two-day transit in the U.S., his first since assuming office, though few details about his itinerary were made public.
item.dc:creator: TAIJING WU and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: More than 100 arrested as Georgian police clash with protesters over suspension of EU talks
item.description: More than 100 demonstrators were arrested overnight in Georgia as protesters clashed with police following the government’s decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union, the country’s Interior Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: Sophiko Megrelidze, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Iceland votes for a new parliament amid disagreements on immigration, energy policy and the economy
item.description: Icelanders are electing a new parliament after disagreements over immigration, energy policy and the economy forced Prime Minister Bjarni Benediktsson to pull the plug on his coalition government and call early elections.
item.dc:creator: Marco Di Marco And Danica Kirka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In Bolivia's scrappy highlands, proud Indigenous Cholas take the runway by storm
item.description: In the huddled market stalls, sprawling farms and pulsing parties of Viacha, a scrappy town southeast of Bolivia’s capital, it’s typical for women to sport bowler hats, tiered skirts and fringed shawls.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Debre And Juan Karita, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: North Korea's Kim vows steadfast support for Russia’s war in Ukraine
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un vowed his country will “invariably support” Russia’s war in Ukraine as he met Russia's defense chief, the North’s state media reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Fishers at a Lebanese port hope ceasefire with Israel means normal life is returning
item.description: The ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah brought hope for normality back to many in southern Lebanon on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Romania's parliamentary vote risks being overshadowed by presidential race chaos
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Hundreds of protesters gathered in Bucharest after a far-right populist unexpectedly won the first round of a presidential race, plunging Romania into turmoil just days before the country holds parliamentary elections. “Better to be dead than a fascist,” one placard reads.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about sudden rebel gains in Syria's 13-year war and why it matters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The 13-year civil war in Syria has roared back into prominence with a surprise rebel offensive on Aleppo, one of Syria's largest cities and an ancient business hub.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgian protesters clash with police for a second night after EU talks are suspended
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Thousands of demonstrators protesting the Georgian government's decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union rallied outside the parliament and clashed with police for a second straight night on Friday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about shock offensive on Syria's largest city
item.description: Syrian rebels against the country's President Bashar Al-Assad advanced toward one of the largest cities in the country — the first time since 2016 — following a shock offensive launched by insurgents on Wednesday, the Associated Press reported on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: What Trump's Russia-Ukraine envoy has said about ending the war
item.description: President-elect Trump's choice for special envoy to the Russia-Ukraine war, Keith Kellogg, has offered fairly detailed proposals for ending the war that began almost three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Ireland headed for coalition government following parliamentary election, exit poll suggests
item.description: DUBLIN (AP) — An exit poll in Ireland’s parliamentary election released late Friday suggests the three biggest parties have won roughly equal shares and the country is headed for another coalition government. A poll released as voting ended at 10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PETER MORRISON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ireland votes in a close-run election where incumbents hope to cling on to power
item.description: Ireland is voting in a parliamentary election that will determine the country's next government; voters are selecting more than 170 lawmakers to sit in the Dail.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Enraged passenger unloads flurry of punches on airport worker, wild video shows
item.description: A furious airline passenger was captured on dramatic video unleashing a flurry of punches on an airport worker over an apparent check-in delay dispute.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: 2 children and a woman crushed to death outside Gaza bakery amid food shortage
item.description: Two children and a woman were crushed to death Friday as a crowd of Palestinians pushed to get bread at a bakery in the Gaza Strip amid a worsening food crisis in the war-ravaged territory, medical officials said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stowaway who boarded New York-to-Paris flight will be sent back to US
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Authorities are investigating after a Russian national who did not have a ticket boarded a Paris-bound Delta flight at New York's John F. Kennedy International Airport this week and was arrested when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats sound alarm on West Bank under Trump administration
item.description: House Democrats are sounding early alarms that a second Trump administration will lead to a vast erosion of Palestinian rights and undermine efforts to bring peace to the volatile Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: Millions in Ukraine without power after Russia attacks grid
item.description: “Attacks on energy facilities are happening all over Ukraine,” Energy Minister Herman Halushchenko said on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Biden admin urges Ukraine to lower draft age to conscript 18-year-olds
item.description: The Biden administration is urging Ukraine to lower its draft age to conscript 18-year-olds to keep pace with Russia’s growing military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden readies $725 million arms aid package for Ukraine: Reuters
item.description: The formal notification to Congress of the weapons package could come as soon as Monday, one official said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps retired General Keith Kellogg for Ukraine envoy role
item.description: While there is currently no special envoy for the war in Ukraine, Trump had privately expressed interest in creating the position.
item.dc:creator: Gram Slattery, Svea Herbst-Bayliss and Steve Holland / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: China releases 3 Americans in prisoner swap
item.description: Mark Swidan, Kai Li and John Leung were freed after years of talks between U.S. and Chinese officials.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Israel ramps up strikes on Beirut as its leadership prepares to vote on ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: The Israeli military also issued warnings for 20 more buildings in Beirut's suburbs to evacuate before they too were struck
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Top EU diplomat says Israel has 'no excuses' to refuse a ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — The European Union’s top diplomat said Tuesday there were “no excuses” for Israel to refuse to accept a ceasefire with the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, saying all its security concerns had been addressed in the U.S.-French-brokered deal.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Two senators urge FIFA not to pick Saudi Arabia as 2034 host
item.description: Democrats Ron Wyden of Oregon and Dick Durbin of Illinois have told FIFA president Gianni Infantino in a letter “we urge you to seek out a host country with a record of upholding human rights.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation
